task:

id: rule-sandbox-lifecycle-sdk
name: sandbox-lifecycle-sdk-first
description: Daytona sandbox create/bootstrap/exec/delete must use @daytona/sdk or AsyncDaytona, never provider.sh.
category: infrastructure
status: active
surrealql_assertion: sandbox_provider == 'daytona' AND sdk_path == true AND shell_provider_sh == false
midspiral:
  tool: verify-rules
  engines: [business]

rule_specs: sandbox lifecycle rule must forbid provider.sh and require daytona sdk
rule_codes:
id: rule-sandbox-lifecycle-sdk
name: sandbox-lifecycle-sdk-first
description: Daytona sandbox create/bootstrap/exec/delete must use @daytona/sdk or AsyncDaytona, never provider.sh.
surrealql_assertion: sandbox_provider == 'daytona' AND sdk_path == true AND shell_provider_sh == false
midspiral:
  tool: verify-rules
validation.validation_cmd: test -f formal/midspiral/rule-sandbox-lifecycle.yaml && grep -q 'rule-sandbox-lifecycle-sdk' formal/midspiral/rule-sandbox-lifecycle.yaml && grep -q 'provider.sh' formal/midspiral/rule-sandbox-lifecycle.yaml && grep -q 'sdk_path == true' formal/midspiral/rule-sandbox-lifecycle.yaml && grep -q 'midspiral:' formal/midspiral/rule-sandbox-lifecycle.yaml && grep -q 'verify-rules' formal/midspiral/rule-sandbox-lifecycle.yaml

pr_title: formal: add Midspiral sandbox-lifecycle business rule

rule_specs: Quint counter module must define Init Inc Next and NonNeg

task:
Write the following file to scripts/verify_midspiral_rules.sh (exact content), then chmod +x it:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
RULE=${1:-formal/midspiral/rule-sandbox-lifecycle.yaml}
if [[ ! -f "$RULE" ]]; then
  echo "MIDSPIRAL_MISS: missing $RULE" >&2
  exit 1
fi
grep -q '^id:' "$RULE"
grep -q 'surrealql_assertion:' "$RULE"
grep -q 'midspiral:' "$RULE"
echo MIDSPIRAL_OK

rule_specs: verify_midspiral_rules.sh must print MIDSPIRAL_OK when rule fields present
rule_codes:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
grep -q '^id:' "$RULE"
grep -q 'surrealql_assertion:' "$RULE"
grep -q 'midspiral:' "$RULE"
echo MIDSPIRAL_OK
validation.validation_cmd: test -f scripts/verify_midspiral_rules.sh && grep -q MIDSPIRAL_OK scripts/verify_midspiral_rules.sh && grep -q surrealql_assertion scripts/verify_midspiral_rules.sh && mkdir -p formal/midspiral && printf '%s\n' 'id: rule-tmp' 'surrealql_assertion: true' 'midspiral:' '  tool: verify-rules' > formal/midspiral/.tmp-rule.yaml && chmod +x scripts/verify_midspiral_rules.sh && ./scripts/verify_midspiral_rules.sh formal/midspiral/.tmp-rule.yaml | grep -q MIDSPIRAL_OK && rm -f formal/midspiral/.tmp-rule.yaml

pr_title: formal: add Midspiral rule verify smoke script
pr_body: Formal validation expansion: shell helper that asserts Midspiral rule YAML shape and prints MIDSPIRAL_OK.

task:
Write the following file to docs/formal-validation-midspiral-quint.md (exact content):

# Formal validation — Midspiral + Quint

## Midspiral

Business rules live under `formal/midspiral/*.yaml` and are checked with `scripts/verify_midspiral_rules.sh` (asserts `id`, `surrealql_assertion`, `midspiral` keys).

## Quint.sh

Executable specs live under `formal/quint/*.qnt`. Validate with:

```bash
quint typecheck formal/quint/counter.qnt
# or
npx @informalsystems/quint typecheck formal/quint/mutex.qnt
```

## Batch contract

SDLC batch jobs set `validation.validation_cmd` as the source of truth (exit 0 required).

rule_specs: formal docs must mention Midspiral Quint and validation_cmd
rule_codes:
# Formal validation — Midspiral + Quint
## Midspiral
## Quint.sh
validation.validation_cmd
validation.validation_cmd: test -f docs/formal-validation-midspiral-quint.md && grep -q 'Midspiral' docs/formal-validation-midspiral-quint.md && grep -q 'Quint.sh' docs/formal-validation-midspiral-quint.md && grep -q 'formal/midspiral' docs/formal-validation-midspiral-quint.md && grep -q 'formal/quint' docs/formal-validation-midspiral-quint.md && grep -q 'validation_cmd' docs/formal-validation-midspiral-quint.md && grep -q 'verify_midspiral_rules' docs/formal-validation-midspiral-quint.md

pr_body: Formal validation expansion: documentation for Midspiral rule YAML and Quint.sh typecheck workflow.

  required_fields: [metric_id, defensibility, source]

task:
Write the following file to scripts/formal_midspiral_quint_smoke.sh (exact content), then chmod +x it:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)
QNT=${1:-$ROOT/formal/quint/smoke.qnt}
if [[ ! -f "$QNT" ]]; then
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$QNT")"
  cat > "$QNT" <<'Q'
module smoke {
  var n: int
  action Init = { n' = 1 }
  action Next = { n' = n }
  val Positive = n > 0
}
Q
fi
grep -q 'module smoke' "$QNT"
if command -v quint >/dev/null; then
  quint typecheck "$QNT"
else
  npx --yes @informalsystems/quint@0.32.0 typecheck "$QNT"
fi
echo FORMAL_SMOKE_OK

rule_specs: formal smoke script must typecheck Quint and print FORMAL_SMOKE_OK
validation.validation_cmd: chmod +x scripts/formal_midspiral_quint_smoke.sh && ./scripts/formal_midspiral_quint_smoke.sh | grep -q FORMAL_SMOKE_OK && test -f formal/quint/smoke.qnt && grep -q 'module smoke' formal/quint/smoke.qnt

pr_body: Formal validation expansion: smoke script that materializes a tiny Quint module, typechecks it, and prints FORMAL_SMOKE_OK.
